prometheusUrl.description: Base URL of the Prometheus instance (e.g. https://prometheus-k8s.openshift-monitoring.svc:9091)

bearerToken.description: Optional bearer token used to authenticate to Prometheus. Takes precedence over the in-cluster service account token; mainly useful for local development against a port-forwarded OpenShift Prometheus, which is not reachable from outside the cluster with the pod's own SA token.

chargeMode.description: Which resource signal to bill against. 'usage' = actual consumption, 'requests' = scheduler reservations, 'limits' = hard caps, 'max' = higher of usage vs requests (default). 'max' is recommended as it discourages over-requesting.

windowHours.description: Lookback window in hours for live cost queries (default: 24)

retentionDays.description: Number of days to retain cost snapshots in the database (default: 90)

rollupAfterDays.description: Hourly snapshot rows older than this many days are aggregated into monthly rollups and removed from the snapshots table (ADR-05). Default: 30. MUST be less than retentionDays — if rollupAfterDays >= retentionDays, pruneOldSnapshots will hard-delete hourly rows before they reach the rollup cutoff, causing silent data loss. Set to 0 to roll up all existing snapshots immediately on the next scheduler cycle (useful for local testing).

cpuCostPerCorePerHour.description: Cost per CPU core per hour in USD (e.g. 0.048)

memoryCostPerGBPerHour.description: Cost per GiB of memory per hour in USD (e.g. 0.006)

gpuCostPerGpuPerHour.description: Cost per GPU (fractional GPU-hours) per hour in USD. Set to 0 (default) when no GPU workloads run on the cluster. Example: 2.48 for an AWS p3.2xlarge V100 GPU fraction.
